enrichment.short_description: High-performance, low-latency NoSQL database compatible with Apache Cassandra and Amazon DynamoDB APIs, built in C++ for predictable performance at scale.
enrichment.pricing_model: Open source core with paid Enterprise (self-managed) and fully managed Cloud tiers
enrichment.starting_price: Free (open source); ScyllaDB Cloud and Enterprise pricing available on request
enrichment.headquarters: Palo Alto, California, USA, with a major research and development office in Tel Aviv, Israel
enrichment.seo_title: ScyllaDB Review 2026: Pricing, Features and NoSQL Database Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ScyllaDB is a Cassandra and DynamoDB compatible NoSQL database built in C++. See 2026 pricing, features, pros, cons and how it compares for high throughput apps.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ScyllaDB is a high-performance, distributed NoSQL database originally developed by Cloudius Systems, a company founded by former Red Hat and KVM engineers Avi Kivity and Dor Laor, which was renamed ScyllaDB and open sourced its core database around 2015. Written in C++ using the Seastar shard-per-core framework, ScyllaDB is designed as a drop-in, wire-compatible replacement for Apache Cassandra and offers a native DynamoDB-compatible API called Alternator, letting teams run DynamoDB style workloads outside of AWS without rewriting application code. The company is headquartered in Palo Alto, California, with a significant engineering presence in Tel Aviv, Israel, and a distributed team reported to work across roughly two dozen countries; employee counts from third party sources range from about 150 to 230 people. ScyllaDB is offered in three ways: a free, community-supported Open Source edition available on GitHub under the AGPL license; a self-managed Enterprise edition for on-premises or private cloud deployments that adds premium features such as workload prioritization, encryption at rest, and dedicated support with custom pricing; and ScyllaDB Cloud (marketed as ScyllaDB X Cloud), a fully managed database-as-a-service running on AWS and Google Cloud Platform with Standard, Professional, and Premium support tiers offering response times ranging from two hours down to fifteen minutes for the most critical issues. None of the paid tiers publish flat list prices; ScyllaDB Cloud billing is based on provisioned cluster resources such as instance type, storage, and service plan, and prospective customers use an online calculator or contact sales for a quote. The database is aimed at data-intensive applications that need high throughput and predictable low-latency performance, and publicly named users and case studies include Discord, Disney+, Expedia, Zillow, Palo Alto Networks, and Tripadvisor. Because it retains close wire compatibility with Cassandra's CQL and Cassandra Query Language drivers, plus optional DynamoDB API compatibility, ScyllaDB is commonly evaluated by engineering teams that are hitting latency, cost, or operational scaling limits with either of those two systems and want a lower-latency alternative without a full data model rewrite. For an SEO directory or comparison context, the key verifiable facts to anchor on are the founding around 2014 to 2015 out of the earlier Cloudius Systems entity, the Palo Alto and Tel Aviv dual headquarters structure, the three-tier pricing model spanning free open source, self-managed Enterprise, and managed Cloud, and the DynamoDB and Cassandra compatibility that differentiates it from most other NoSQL databases in its category.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ScyllaDB is a distributed NoSQL database written in C++ that positions itself as a faster, more resource-efficient alternative to Apache Cassandra and Amazon DynamoDB. It grew out of Cloudius Systems, a company started by former Red Hat KVM engineers Avi Kivity and Dor Laor, which was renamed ScyllaDB around 2014 and released its core database as open source software the following year.

The company is headquartered in Palo Alto, California, with a substantial engineering office in Tel Aviv, Israel, and a globally distributed workforce. ScyllaDB targets data-intensive applications such as messaging, ad tech, gaming, IoT, and financial services that require consistently low latency at very high throughput.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ScyllaDB's core architectural advantage is its shard-per-core design built on the Seastar framework, which lets each CPU core operate largely independently to avoid the lock contention and garbage collection pauses that affect JVM-based databases like Cassandra. It maintains wire compatibility with Cassandra's CQL, so existing Cassandra drivers and, in many cases, application code can connect with minimal changes.

A distinguishing feature is Alternator, ScyllaDB's DynamoDB-compatible API, which allows teams running DynamoDB workloads to move them to ScyllaDB, whether self-managed or in ScyllaDB Cloud, without rewriting their data access layer. ScyllaDB Cloud adds fully managed operations, automated backups, monitoring, and multi-region active-active deployment options on AWS and Google Cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ScyllaDB Open Source is free and community-supported. ScyllaDB Enterprise, for self-managed deployments on-premises or in private cloud, adds premium features and dedicated support but requires contacting sales for a quote. ScyllaDB Cloud is billed based on provisioned resources, instance type, storage, and the chosen support tier, with Standard, Professional, and Premium plans that differ mainly in support response times and advanced networking or security options; no flat public price list is available and customers use ScyllaDB's online pricing calculator or talk to sales for an estimate.

features.title: Shard-per-core architecture
features.description: Built on the Seastar C++ framework, ScyllaDB assigns each CPU core its own memory, CPU, and I/O, avoiding cross-core coordination overhead common in JVM-based databases.

features.title: Cassandra CQL compatibility
features.description: ScyllaDB is wire-compatible with Apache Cassandra's query language and many existing drivers, easing migration for teams already running Cassandra.

features.title: DynamoDB-compatible API (Alternator)
features.description: A native DynamoDB-compatible API lets teams run DynamoDB-style workloads on ScyllaDB anywhere, including outside AWS, without changing application code.

features.title: Fully managed ScyllaDB Cloud
features.description: A database-as-a-service offering on AWS and Google Cloud Platform that handles provisioning, backups, monitoring, and scaling.

features.title: Predictable low-latency performance
features.description: Designed for consistent p99 latency under high load, which ScyllaDB markets as its core differentiator versus DynamoDB and Cassandra.

features.title: Multi-region active-active deployment
features.description: Professional and Premium ScyllaDB Cloud tiers support active-active replication across regions for high availability.

features.title: Open source core
features.description: The base database is available as free, community-supported open source software on GitHub under the AGPL license.

features.title: Enterprise-grade security options
features.description: Higher Cloud tiers add bring-your-own encryption key support and advanced networking controls for regulated environments.

pros: Consistently cited by users as significantly lower latency than Cassandra under heavy load
DynamoDB-compatible API can meaningfully reduce cloud database costs for high-throughput workloads
Free, actively maintained open source edition with no licensing cost for self-managed use
Fully managed Cloud option removes operational burden for teams that do not want to self-host
Drop-in compatibility with existing Cassandra tooling and drivers eases migration
cons: No public flat-rate pricing for Enterprise or Cloud tiers, requiring a sales conversation to get a quote
Operating a self-managed cluster still requires distributed systems expertise
Smaller ecosystem and third-party tooling compared to long-established Cassandra or DynamoDB
AGPL licensing on the open source edition can raise compliance questions for some organizations
Migrating fully off Cassandra or DynamoDB still requires careful testing despite compatibility layers

faqs.question: Is ScyllaDB free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. ScyllaDB Open Source is free and community-supported. Paid options are ScyllaDB Enterprise for self-managed deployments and ScyllaDB Cloud for a fully managed service, both priced on request.

faqs.question: Is ScyllaDB compatible with Apache Cassandra?
faqs.answer: Yes. ScyllaDB is wire-compatible with Cassandra's CQL query language, so many existing Cassandra drivers and applications can connect to ScyllaDB with little or no code changes.

faqs.question: Can ScyllaDB replace Amazon DynamoDB?
faqs.answer: ScyllaDB offers Alternator, a native DynamoDB-compatible API, so DynamoDB workloads can run on ScyllaDB, either self-managed or on ScyllaDB Cloud, without rewriting application code.

faqs.question: Who founded ScyllaDB?
faqs.answer: ScyllaDB was founded by Avi Kivity and Dor Laor, former Red Hat engineers who previously worked on the KVM hypervisor, originally under the name Cloudius Systems.

faqs.question: Where is ScyllaDB headquartered?
faqs.answer: ScyllaDB is headquartered in Palo Alto, California, USA, with a major research and development office in Tel Aviv, Israel.

faqs.question: What cloud providers does ScyllaDB Cloud support?
faqs.answer: ScyllaDB Cloud, the fully managed database-as-a-service offering, is available on AWS and Google Cloud Platform.

faqs.question: How is ScyllaDB Cloud priced?
faqs.answer: ScyllaDB Cloud billing is based on provisioned cluster resources such as instance type, storage, and the selected support plan tier; ScyllaDB does not publish flat per-hour rates and directs prospects to a pricing calculator or sales team.

faqs.question: What companies use ScyllaDB?
faqs.answer: Publicly referenced ScyllaDB users include Discord, Disney+, Expedia, Zillow, Palo Alto Networks, and Tripadvisor.

use_cases.title: Real-time messaging and notification systems
use_cases.description: Applications like chat and notification platforms use ScyllaDB for its ability to sustain very high write throughput with predictable low latency at scale.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams building consumer-scale messaging or social platforms

use_cases.title: Migrating off DynamoDB to control costs
use_cases.description: Teams facing high or unpredictable DynamoDB bills use ScyllaDB's Alternator API to move workloads to self-managed or ScyllaDB Cloud infrastructure without a full data layer rewrite.
use_cases.audience: Platform and infrastructure teams managing AWS cost efficiency

use_cases.title: Replacing Cassandra for lower latency
use_cases.description: Organizations running large Cassandra clusters that experience garbage collection pauses or tail latency issues evaluate ScyllaDB as a wire-compatible, lower-latency replacement.
use_cases.audience: Database and SRE teams operating large-scale Cassandra deployments

features: Premium features such as workload prioritization
Fully managed on AWS and GCP

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one SEO platform offering keyword rank tracking, site audits, keyword research, backlink monitoring, and white-label reporting for agencies and in-house marketers.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered SaaS subscription with a 14-day free trial
enrichment.starting_price: From approximately 87.20 euros per month (Core plan, billed annually)
enrichment.headquarters: Wilmington, Delaware, USA, with international offices
enrichment.seo_title: SE Ranking Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Plans Compared
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SE Ranking is an all-in-one SEO toolset for rank tracking, audits, keyword research and backlinks. Compare 2026 pricing plans, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SE Ranking is an all-in-one search engine optimization and digital marketing software platform that was founded in 2012 and has grown from a single rank-tracking tool into a broader suite covering keyword rank tracking, on-page and technical SEO audits, keyword research, competitor keyword analysis, backlink monitoring, and customizable white-label reporting. The company lists a registered headquarters in Wilmington, Delaware, USA, and operates with an internationally distributed team reported at roughly 200 to 220 people as of recent company profiles. SE Ranking is aimed primarily at digital marketing agencies and in-house SEO or marketing teams at small and medium-sized businesses, and third-party review data shows a heavy concentration of reviewers in the marketing and advertising sector describing it as strong value for money compared with higher-priced competitors such as Semrush, Ahrefs, and Moz. Pricing is structured as tiered monthly or annual SaaS subscriptions with a 20 percent discount for annual billing. The Core plan, SE Ranking's entry-level tier, includes 10 projects, one manager seat, tracking of roughly 2,000 keywords per day, around 100 AI-related prompts per day, competitive research covering up to five domains, an audit capacity of about 250,000 pages per month, and roughly 25,000 API credits with MCP access. The Growth plan steps up to 30 projects, three manager seats, 5,000 tracked keywords per day, 250 daily AI prompts, 15 domains for competitive research, 2 million pages per month of audit capacity, 100,000 API credits, and adds historical ranking data, guest collaboration links, page monitoring, and dedicated support. An Enterprise tier offers fully custom limits and pricing negotiated directly with SE Ranking's sales team. Optional add-ons include an Agency Pack, an AI Search add-on, and a standalone API package, each billed separately from the core plans. All paid plans are preceded by a 14-day free trial that does not require a credit card and includes a functional but capped version of the platform, such as up to 10 projects and 750 tracked keywords per day. SE Ranking differentiates itself from larger SEO suites primarily on price, with reviewers frequently citing it as one of the lowest-cost tools in its category that still delivers a comparable breadth of features, making it a common recommendation for budget-conscious agencies and freelancers rather than the largest enterprise marketing departments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SE Ranking is a cloud-based SEO platform founded in 2012 that combines rank tracking, site auditing, keyword research, competitor analysis, and backlink monitoring in a single subscription product. It is positioned as a lower-cost alternative to larger SEO suites while still targeting agencies, freelancers, and in-house marketing teams that need a comprehensive toolset.

The company's listed headquarters is in Wilmington, Delaware, USA, and it operates with an internationally distributed team. SE Ranking markets itself heavily on price-to-feature ratio, and third-party reviews frequently compare it directly with Semrush, Ahrefs, and Moz on cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core functionality includes daily keyword rank tracking across desktop and mobile, geo-targeted rankings down to the city level, a technical and on-page SEO audit tool, keyword research and competitor keyword gap analysis, and backlink monitoring. SE Ranking also offers customizable, white-label reporting with scheduled report delivery, which is popular among agencies managing multiple client accounts.

Newer additions include AI-related features such as daily AI prompt allowances and AI-driven competitive research across a limited number of domains per plan, along with API and MCP access for integrating SE Ranking data into other workflows or AI tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SE Ranking offers three core plans. Core starts at roughly 109 euros per month billed monthly, or about 87.20 euros per month billed annually, and includes 10 projects, 2,000 tracked keywords per day, and 250,000 pages of monthly audit capacity. Growth is priced at roughly 235 euros per month, or about 188 euros per month billed annually, and raises the limits to 30 projects, 5,000 tracked keywords, and 2 million audited pages per month, while adding historical data and dedicated support.

An Enterprise plan offers custom limits and pricing for larger teams. Optional add-ons such as an Agency Pack, an AI Search add-on, and a standalone API package are billed separately. A 14-day free trial with a capped feature set is available on all plans without requiring a credit card.

features.title: Keyword rank tracking
features.description: Daily tracking of keyword positions across desktop and mobile, with geo-targeted rankings down to the city level.

features.title: Website SEO audit
features.description: Automated technical and on-page audits that scan up to millions of pages per month depending on plan, surfacing crawlability and optimization issues.

features.title: Keyword research and competitor analysis
features.description: Keyword discovery tools alongside competitor keyword gap analysis to identify ranking opportunities.

features.title: Backlink monitoring
features.description: Tracks backlink profiles and changes over time to support link building and toxic link identification.

features.title: White-label reporting
features.description: Customizable, brandable reports with scheduled delivery, aimed at agencies reporting to clients.

features.title: AI-assisted research tools
features.description: Daily AI prompt allowances and AI-driven competitive research across a limited number of domains per plan.

features.title: API and MCP access
features.description: Programmatic access to SE Ranking data via API credits, plus MCP access for integrating with AI tooling.

features.title: Multi-seat and guest collaboration
features.description: Manager seats and, on higher plans, guest collaboration links for sharing data with clients or team members.

pros: Frequently cited by reviewers as lower cost than Semrush, Ahrefs, or Moz for comparable functionality
Broad feature set covering rank tracking, audits, keyword research, and backlinks in one subscription
White-label reporting is well suited to agencies managing multiple clients
14-day free trial available without requiring a credit card
Geo-targeted rank tracking down to the city level
cons: Enterprise pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Entry-level Core plan has meaningful caps on projects, keywords, and API credits
Some advanced features such as historical data and dedicated support are reserved for the Growth plan and above
Add-ons like Agency Pack and AI Search increase total cost beyond the base plan price
Brand recognition and third-party integration ecosystem are smaller than category leaders like Semrush or Ahrefs

faqs.question: How much does SE Ranking cost?
faqs.answer: SE Ranking's Core plan starts at roughly 109 euros per month billed monthly, or about 87.20 euros per month billed annually. The Growth plan is roughly 235 euros per month, or about 188 euros per month billed annually. Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does SE Ranking offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes. SE Ranking offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required, giving access to a capped version of the platform including up to 10 projects and 750 tracked keywords per day.

faqs.question: When was SE Ranking founded?
faqs.answer: SE Ranking was founded in 2012.

faqs.question: Where is SE Ranking headquartered?
faqs.answer: SE Ranking's registered headquarters is in Wilmington, Delaware, USA, and the company operates with an internationally distributed team.

faqs.question: Is SE Ranking suitable for agencies?
faqs.answer: Yes. SE Ranking includes white-label, customizable reporting with scheduled delivery and, on higher plans, guest collaboration links, both of which are commonly used by agencies managing multiple client accounts.

faqs.question: How does SE Ranking compare to Semrush or Ahrefs?
faqs.answer: Reviewers frequently describe SE Ranking as offering comparable core SEO functionality, such as rank tracking, audits, and backlink monitoring, at a lower price point than Semrush or Ahrefs.

faqs.question: Does SE Ranking have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes. Paid plans include a set number of API credits, and a standalone API add-on is available for additional usage.

faqs.question: What is included in the Growth plan that is not in Core?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan adds historical ranking data, guest collaboration links, page monitoring, and dedicated support on top of higher limits for projects, tracked keywords, and audit capacity.

use_cases.description: Marketers use SE Ranking's competitor keyword gap analysis and backlink monitoring tools to identify content and link building opportunities.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hostable file sync and share platform with a free Community Edition and a paid Professional Edition offering enterprise administration, audit logs, and clustering.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium open source, with a paid per-user Professional Edition license
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Community Edition); Professional Edition from 100 US dollars total for up to 9 users
enrichment.company_size: Small, globally distributed team (exact headcount not publicly disclosed)
enrichment.seo_title: Seafile Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Self-Hosted File Sync Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Seafile is an open-source file sync and share platform. See 2026 pricing for the free Community Edition and paid Professional Edition, plus key features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Seafile is an open-source file synchronization and sharing platform founded in 2012 by Daniel Pan, who serves as CEO, and Jonathan Xu, who serves as CTO, after the pair began developing sync-and-share concepts as graduate students around 2009. The company behind Seafile operates as a globally distributed team with offices in Beijing, China, and Singapore, backed by Seafile Ltd. and SeaCloud Labs Pte. Ltd., and its public-facing website is offered in Chinese, English, and German. Seafile is distinguished from consumer cloud storage services by being self-hostable: organizations can run it on their own servers or private cloud infrastructure rather than relying solely on a third-party hosted service, which appeals to privacy-conscious teams, educational institutions, and public sector organizations that need to keep data under their own control. The product is split into two editions. The Community Edition is free and open source, providing core file sync and share functionality, desktop and mobile clients, and basic web-based file management suitable for individuals, small teams, and self-hosters comfortable managing their own server. The Professional Edition is a commercially licensed upgrade aimed at enterprise environments, adding an advanced admin console for user and group management, detailed audit logs that track logins, file access, updates and deletions, integration with LDAP, Active Directory and SAML for centralized authentication, and server clustering for high-availability deployments. As of the most recent published pricing, the Professional Edition is free for up to 3 users, available at a flat rate of 100 US dollars total for up to 9 users, and then scales per user starting around 48 US dollars per user for 10 to 249 users, decreasing progressively for larger user counts down to roughly 35 US dollars per user for 750 to 999 users, with custom quotes for deployments of 1,000 or more users; educational institutions receive a 50 percent discount and public sector entities receive 25 percent off. Because Seafile is self-hosted, total cost of ownership also depends on the customer's own server and storage infrastructure, unlike fully hosted SaaS file sync competitors. Seafile is commonly evaluated by IT teams and system administrators alongside other self-hosted file sync tools such as Nextcloud and ownCloud, and its main differentiators are cited as sync performance, block-level deduplication, and a lighter-weight architecture compared to some of those alternatives.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Seafile is an open-source file synchronization and sharing platform founded in 2012 by Daniel Pan and Jonathan Xu, who began developing the underlying sync-and-share concepts as graduate students several years earlier. The company operates as a globally distributed team with offices in Beijing, China, and Singapore.

Unlike consumer cloud storage services, Seafile is designed to be self-hosted on an organization's own servers or private cloud, giving IT teams control over where data lives. It is commonly compared to other self-hosted file sync platforms such as Nextcloud and ownCloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The free Community Edition provides core file synchronization and sharing, desktop and mobile clients, and web-based file management. The paid Professional Edition adds enterprise administration tools including an advanced admin console, detailed audit logs covering logins, file access, updates and deletions, and integration with LDAP, Active Directory and SAML for centralized authentication.

Professional Edition also supports server clustering for high-availability deployments, which is aimed at larger organizations that need Seafile to scale across multiple servers with minimal downtime.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Community Edition is free and open source. The Professional Edition is free for up to 3 users, offered at a flat 100 US dollars total for up to 9 users, and then priced per user starting around 48 US dollars per user for 10 to 249 users, scaling down for larger deployments to roughly 35 US dollars per user for 750 to 999 users.

Custom pricing is available for deployments of 1,000 or more users. Educational institutions receive a 50 percent discount and public sector organizations receive 25 percent off Professional Edition licensing.

features.title: Self-hosted file sync and share
features.description: Runs on an organization's own servers or private cloud, giving full control over where files and data reside.

features.title: Desktop and mobile clients
features.description: Native sync clients for major desktop operating systems and mobile apps for on-the-go file access.

features.title: Block-level deduplication
features.description: Efficient storage and sync performance through block-level file deduplication rather than syncing whole files on every change.

features.title: Advanced admin console (Professional)
features.description: Enterprise-grade user and group management tools available in the paid Professional Edition.

features.title: Audit logging (Professional)
features.description: Detailed logs of logins, file access, updates and deletions for compliance and security monitoring.

features.title: LDAP, Active Directory and SAML integration (Professional)
features.description: Centralized authentication support for enterprise identity systems in the Professional Edition.

features.title: Server clustering (Professional)
features.description: High-availability deployment support that lets Seafile scale across multiple servers.

features.title: File sharing and collaboration links
features.description: Shareable links and permission controls for collaborating on files with internal or external users.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition available for individuals and small teams
Self-hosting gives organizations full control over data location and compliance
Professional Edition pricing includes a free tier for very small teams (up to 3 users)
Discounts available for educational and public sector organizations
Block-level deduplication supports efficient sync performance
cons: Self-hosting requires in-house server management and technical expertise
Enterprise features like audit logs, SAML, and clustering are locked behind the paid Professional Edition
Company size and financials are not publicly disclosed, making vendor stability harder to assess
Total cost of ownership includes separate server and storage infrastructure not covered by the license fee
Smaller ecosystem and community compared to larger self-hosted alternatives like Nextcloud

faqs.question: Is Seafile free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Seafile's Community Edition is free and open source. A paid Professional Edition is available for organizations that need enterprise administration, audit logs, and clustering.

faqs.question: Who founded Seafile?
faqs.answer: Seafile was founded in 2012 by Daniel Pan, who serves as CEO, and Jonathan Xu, who serves as CTO.

faqs.question: Where is Seafile headquartered?
faqs.answer: Seafile operates as a globally distributed team with offices in Beijing, China, and Singapore, backed by Seafile Ltd. and SeaCloud Labs Pte. Ltd.

faqs.question: How much does Seafile Professional Edition cost?
faqs.answer: Professional Edition is free for up to 3 users, 100 US dollars total for up to 9 users, and then scales per user, starting around 48 US dollars per user for 10 to 249 users, with further discounts at larger user counts and custom pricing above 1,000 users.

faqs.question: Can Seafile be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes. Seafile is designed to be self-hosted on an organization's own servers or private cloud infrastructure, which is one of its main differentiators from consumer cloud storage services.

faqs.question: Does Seafile offer discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes. Educational institutions receive a 50 percent discount and public sector entities receive 25 percent off Professional Edition licensing.

faqs.question: How does Seafile compare to Nextcloud?
faqs.answer: Both are open-source, self-hostable file sync platforms. Seafile is often noted by reviewers for its sync performance and block-level deduplication, while Nextcloud offers a broader built-in app ecosystem.

faqs.question: What languages does Seafile support?
faqs.answer: The Seafile website and product interface are offered in Chinese, English, and German.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered B2B sales intelligence platform providing verified contact data, real-time prospecting, and lead generation tools for sales teams.

enrichment.seo_title: Seamless.AI Review 2026: Pricing, Credits and Features Explained
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Seamless.AI is an AI sales lead generation platform. See 2026 pricing for Free, Basic, Pro and Enterprise plans, credit limits, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Seamless.AI is a B2B sales intelligence and lead generation platform founded by CEO Brandon Bornancin, with company profiles most commonly citing 2015 as the founding year, though some sources list dates as early as 2014 or as late as 2018. The company is headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, at 438 East Wilson Bridge Road, and reported employee counts have grown from around 341 in 2023 to roughly 400 as of 2026, according to third-party company data providers. Seamless.AI has raised approximately 75 million US dollars in funding as of a 2026 disclosure and reported annual recurring revenue of about 43.8 million US dollars serving around 200,000 customers, per third-party revenue tracking sources; these figures come from third-party estimates rather than official company disclosures and should be treated as approximate. The core product uses AI and real-time web search to help sales teams find verified business contact information, including direct dials, mobile numbers and email addresses, along with company firmographic data, intent signals, and org chart mapping, positioning it as a competitor to tools like ZoomInfo, Apollo.io, and Cognism. Pricing operates on a credit-based system across four tiers. The Free plan grants 50 lifetime credits with no ongoing subscription. The Basic plan is priced at 147 US dollars per month when billed annually and includes 250 credits per month, aimed at individual users or very small teams. The Pro and Enterprise plans are not published with flat prices and instead require a custom sales quote, because pricing depends on the number of credits, team size, and contract length; third-party pricing trackers report a wide range for Pro, commonly cited between roughly 165 and 475 US dollars per month depending on negotiated terms and seat count, with a typical minimum of 5 user licenses and a strong preference for 12-month annual contracts billed upfront. Enterprise contracts have been reported anywhere from about 20,000 to over 100,000 US dollars annually depending on scale. Multiple independent pricing analyses note that Seamless.AI's list pricing changes frequently and that quoted prices can vary between different prospects contacted in the same month, so buyers are commonly advised to negotiate and compare multiple quotes before committing to an annual contract. For an SEO directory, the most defensible, source-backed facts are the Columbus, Ohio headquarters, the founder Brandon Bornancin, the credit-based Free and Basic pricing tiers with the 147 dollar per month Basic price point, and the quote-only nature of Pro and Enterprise plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Seamless.AI is a B2B sales intelligence platform founded by CEO Brandon Bornancin, with most company profiles citing 2015 as the founding year. The company is headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, and has grown to roughly 400 employees as of recent third-party company data.

The platform uses AI and real-time web search to surface verified contact information, including direct dials and email addresses, along with firmographic and intent data, positioning it against sales intelligence competitors such as ZoomInfo, Apollo.io, and Cognism.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Seamless.AI's core feature is real-time contact search, which uses AI to find and verify direct phone numbers, mobile numbers, and email addresses for target prospects as users search, rather than relying solely on a static pre-built database. It also offers org chart building, intent data, and list-building tools for outbound sales prospecting.

The platform integrates with common CRM and sales engagement tools and offers a browser extension for prospecting directly from LinkedIn and company websites, which sales teams use to build outbound lead lists without leaving their existing workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Seamless.AI uses a credit-based pricing model with four tiers. The Free plan includes 50 lifetime credits at no cost. The Basic plan costs 147 US dollars per month when billed annually and includes 250 credits per month.

Pro and Enterprise plans do not have published flat pricing and require a custom sales quote based on credit volume, seat count, and contract length; Pro typically requires a minimum of 5 user licenses on a 12-month annual contract, while Enterprise contracts can range from the low tens of thousands to well over 100,000 US dollars annually depending on scale.

features.title: Real-time contact search
features.description: Uses AI and live web search to find and verify direct dials, mobile numbers, and email addresses for prospects at the point of search.

features.title: Org chart building
features.description: Maps company organizational structures to help sales reps identify decision-makers and reporting lines.

features.title: Intent data
features.description: Surfaces buyer intent signals to help sales teams prioritize accounts that are actively researching relevant solutions.

features.title: List building and prospecting
features.description: Lets users build targeted prospect lists based on firmographic and demographic filters.

features.title: Browser extension
features.description: A browser extension for prospecting contact data directly from LinkedIn profiles and company websites.

features.title: CRM and sales engagement integrations
features.description: Connects with common CRM and outbound sales engagement platforms to push contact data into existing workflows.

features.title: Credit-based data access
features.description: Contact reveals are metered through a monthly or lifetime credit allowance depending on the plan.

features.title: Data verification
features.description: Contact data is checked and updated in an effort to reduce bounce rates on emails and disconnected phone numbers.

Browser extension makes prospecting from LinkedIn and company sites straightforward

cons: Pro and Enterprise pricing is not published and requires a sales quote, making cost comparison difficult

faqs.question: How much does Seamless.AI cost?
faqs.answer: Seamless.AI offers a Free plan with 50 lifetime credits and a Basic plan at 147 US dollars per month billed annually with 250 credits per month. Pro and Enterprise plans require a custom sales quote.

faqs.question: Who founded Seamless.AI?
faqs.answer: Seamless.AI was founded by CEO Brandon Bornancin. Most company profiles cite 2015 as the founding year, though some sources list dates ranging from 2014 to 2018.

faqs.question: Where is Seamless.AI headquartered?
faqs.answer: Seamless.AI is headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, USA.

faqs.question: Does Seamless.AI have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. Seamless.AI offers a Free plan that includes 50 lifetime credits with no ongoing subscription required.

faqs.question: How is Seamless.AI priced for teams?
faqs.answer: Team-oriented Pro plans typically require a minimum of 5 user licenses and are usually sold as 12-month annual contracts billed upfront, with exact pricing provided via a custom sales quote.

faqs.question: What does Seamless.AI's Enterprise plan cost?
faqs.answer: Enterprise pricing is not published and is quoted individually; third-party sources report contracts ranging from roughly 20,000 to over 100,000 US dollars annually depending on scale and requirements.

faqs.question: What data does Seamless.AI provide?
faqs.answer: Seamless.AI provides verified direct dial and mobile phone numbers, email addresses, company firmographic data, org charts, and buyer intent signals.

faqs.question: How many customers does Seamless.AI have?
faqs.answer: Third-party revenue tracking sources report Seamless.AI serves around 200,000 customers, though this figure comes from third-party estimates rather than official company disclosure.

enrichment.seo_title: SeaTable Review 2026: Pricing, Features and No-Code Database Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeaTable is a no-code spreadsheet-database hybrid hosted in Germany. See 2026 pricing for Free, Plus, and Enterprise plans, plus key features and use cases.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SeaTable is a collaborative, no-code database and spreadsheet hybrid platform founded in 2020 as a joint venture between Seafile Ltd., a software company based in Guangzhou, China, and SeaTable GmbH, headquartered in Mainz, Germany. The company was founded by Dr. Ralf Dyllick-Brenzinger and Christoph Dyllick-Brenzinger, and its German entity emphasizes data protection by storing SeaTable Cloud data exclusively in German data centers that are monitored and regularly backed up, which the company markets as a differentiator for European customers with strict data residency or GDPR requirements. The product functions similarly to other no-code spreadsheet-database tools in its category, letting users build custom tables, views, forms, and automations without traditional coding, and it supports scripting extensions through Python for more advanced customization. SeaTable offers both a cloud-hosted SaaS product and on-premises server licenses for organizations that want to self-host. The cloud pricing structure has three main published tiers plus a custom Dedicated Cloud option. The Free plan supports up to 25 users at no cost, with limits of 10,000 rows, 2 gigabytes of storage, one month of snapshot history, 100 Python script runs, 3,000 API calls, and 12.5 AI credits, alongside unlimited bases and tables within those row and storage limits. The Plus plan supports unlimited users at 7 euros per user per month when billed annually, or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly, and raises limits to 50,000 rows, 50 gigabytes of storage, six months of snapshot history, 250 Python script runs per user, 10,000 API calls per user, and 50 AI credits. The Enterprise plan costs 14 euros per user per month billed annually, or 18 euros per user per month billed monthly, and includes unlimited rows, 100 gigabytes of storage, 12 months of snapshot history, 1,000 Python script runs per user, 50,000 API calls per user, 500 AI credits, advanced customization options, and SAML single sign-on. A Dedicated Cloud plan requires a minimum of 100 users and offers unlimited rows, 1,000 gigabytes of storage, 24 months of snapshot history, and unlimited Python runs, API calls, and automations, with custom pricing available on request. On-premises SeaTable Server licenses are also available for organizations of 3, 10, 25, or 50-plus users on 1, 2, or 3-year terms, with discounts for longer commitments. SeaTable is commonly compared to other no-code database platforms such as Airtable and Baserow, with its Germany-based data hosting and GDPR-oriented positioning cited most often as its key differentiator for European buyers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SeaTable is a no-code database and spreadsheet hybrid platform founded in 2020 as a joint venture between Seafile Ltd. of Guangzhou, China, and SeaTable GmbH, headquartered in Mainz, Germany, founded by Dr. Ralf Dyllick-Brenzinger and Christoph Dyllick-Brenzinger.

The platform is positioned around European data protection standards, storing SeaTable Cloud data exclusively in German data centers, which appeals to organizations with GDPR or data residency requirements. It is commonly compared to other no-code table-database tools such as Airtable.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SeaTable lets users build custom tables, views, and forms without traditional coding, and supports automations and Python scripting for advanced customization. Higher tiers add API access, AI credits for AI-assisted features, and longer snapshot retention for restoring earlier versions of a base.

Enterprise and Dedicated Cloud tiers add advanced customization options and SAML single sign-on, aimed at larger organizations needing centralized identity management and higher usage limits for rows, storage, and automations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SeaTable's Free plan supports up to 25 users with 10,000 rows and 2 gigabytes of storage at no cost. The Plus plan is 7 euros per user per month billed annually, or 9 euros per user per month billed monthly, supporting unlimited users with higher row, storage, and API limits.

The Enterprise plan costs 14 euros per user per month billed annually, or 18 euros per user per month billed monthly, and adds SAML single sign-on and advanced customization. A Dedicated Cloud plan for 100-plus users and on-premises SeaTable Server licenses are available with custom pricing.

faqs.answer: SeaTable's Free plan supports up to 25 users at no cost. The Plus plan is 7 euros per user per month billed annually, and the Enterprise plan is 14 euros per user per month billed annually.

faqs.question: Who owns SeaTable?
faqs.answer: SeaTable is a joint venture between Seafile Ltd., based in Guangzhou, China, and SeaTable GmbH, headquartered in Mainz, Germany, founded by Dr. Ralf Dyllick-Brenzinger and Christoph Dyllick-Brenzinger.

faqs.question: Where is SeaTable data stored?
faqs.answer: SeaTable Cloud data is processed and stored exclusively in German data centers, which are monitored and regularly backed up.

faqs.question: Does SeaTable offer on-premises hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeaTable Server licenses are available for organizations of 3, 10, 25, or 50-plus users on 1, 2, or 3-year terms for self-hosted deployments.

faqs.question: When was SeaTable founded?
faqs.answer: SeaTable was founded in 2020.

faqs.answer: Yes. SeaTable includes automation tools to trigger actions based on data changes, plus Python scripting for more advanced customization.

faqs.question: What is SeaTable's Enterprise plan good for?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan adds SAML single sign-on, advanced customization, unlimited rows, and higher API and AI credit allowances, aimed at larger organizations.

faqs.question: How does SeaTable compare to Airtable?
faqs.answer: SeaTable is frequently compared to Airtable as a no-code database and spreadsheet hybrid, with SeaTable's main differentiator being its Germany-based data hosting and GDPR-oriented positioning.

enrichment.seo_title: Seatsurfing Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Desk Booking Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Seatsurfing is open-source desk and room booking software for hybrid offices. See 2026 pricing for self-hosted, Free, Professional and Enterprise tiers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Seatsurfing is open-source desk sharing, room reservation, and hybrid workplace booking software developed by Seatsurfing GmbH, a German company based in Friedrichsdorf, managed by Jan Jonas and Heinrich Beck. The software is published as open source on GitHub under the seatsurfing organization and is designed to let organizations manage flexible workspaces, hot desking, meeting room reservations, and free seating arrangements through a modern web interface with visual floor plan support. Because the core product is open source, organizations can self-host Seatsurfing on their own infrastructure entirely free of charge, with unlimited users and access to all core features, which distinguishes it from many commercial desk-booking SaaS competitors that charge per seat regardless of deployment size. Alongside the self-hosted option, Seatsurfing GmbH also operates a fully managed, cloud-hosted SaaS version with tiered pricing. The hosted Free plan supports up to 10 users at no cost and includes most of Seatsurfing's features, such as Microsoft Teams integration, Microsoft Exchange synchronization, Atlassian Confluence integration, and managed cloud hosting located in Germany, without requiring a credit card to start. The Professional plan removes the user cap and is priced at 0.90 euros per user per month, adding booking approvals and restrictions, recurring bookings, support for custom domains, third-party identity provider integration, a kiosk mode for on-site check-in displays, and 24/7 support; prospective customers can trial the Professional plan free for 30 days without commitment. An Enterprise plan is available for organizations with a minimum of 500 users and offers dedicated infrastructure hosted in either Germany or Finland, maximum data protection guarantees, and 24/7 priority support, with pricing negotiated individually. The self-hosted deployment option also has optional paid support available for organizations that want commercial assistance while still running the open-source software on their own servers. Seatsurfing integrates with workplace collaboration tools including Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Exchange, and Atlassian Confluence, positioning it as a workplace management platform for hybrid offices that need to coordinate desk sharing, room bookings, and shared space allocation as employees split time between office and remote work. Precise founding year and detailed company size information for Seatsurfing GmbH are not consistently published in public sources, so those data points should be treated as unconfirmed rather than stated as fact.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Seatsurfing is open-source desk sharing, room reservation, and hybrid workplace booking software developed by Seatsurfing GmbH, a company based in Friedrichsdorf, Germany, managed by Jan Jonas and Heinrich Beck. The software is published openly on GitHub and can be self-hosted at no cost, or used through a fully managed cloud-hosted SaaS version.
Seatsurfing is designed for organizations managing hybrid office setups, offering hot desking, meeting room reservations, free seating, and visual floor plan support through a web-based interface.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core booking functionality includes desk and room reservations, visual floor plans, and hot desking support for flexible seating. Integrations are available with Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Exchange, and Atlassian Confluence, letting employees book workspace directly from tools they already use.

Paid tiers add booking approvals and restrictions, recurring bookings, custom domains, third-party identity provider support, and a kiosk mode for on-site check-in displays, along with 24/7 support on the Professional and Enterprise plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Seatsurfing can be self-hosted for free with unlimited users and all core features under its open-source license, with optional paid support available. The hosted SaaS Free plan supports up to 10 users at no cost with most features included.

The hosted Professional plan is priced at 0.90 euros per user per month with unlimited users and adds features like recurring bookings and 24/7 support, with a 30-day free trial. The Enterprise plan requires a minimum of 500 users and offers dedicated infrastructure in Germany or Finland at custom pricing.

enrichment.short_description: Fast, open-source distributed storage system for S3-compatible object storage, files, and data lakes, designed to handle billions of files with O(1) disk access.
enrichment.pricing_model: Open source (Apache 2.0 license) free for self-hosting, with a paid per-terabyte Enterprise edition
enrichment.starting_price: Free (open source, or under 25 terabytes for development and testing); Enterprise from 2 US dollars per terabyte per month
enrichment.seo_title: SeaweedFS Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Distributed Storage Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeaweedFS is an open-source distributed storage system for S3-compatible object storage. See 2026 Enterprise pricing per terabyte, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SeaweedFS is a fast, open-source distributed storage system created by Chris Lu, who has publicly maintained the project since around 2015 and previously spent 11 years as an engineer at Uber before later joining poolside.ai. The project is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, is hosted on GitHub under the seaweedfs organization, and has accumulated roughly 32,000 stars, reflecting significant developer community adoption as a self-hosted alternative to commercial object storage. SeaweedFS is designed for object storage compatible with the S3 API, general-purpose file systems, and, more recently, Apache Iceberg table storage, and it is built to handle billions of files efficiently through an architecture that keeps disk seeks to O(1), meaning file access typically requires only a single disk read operation regardless of how many files are stored. Its architecture separates concerns between a lightweight central master, which tracks only storage volumes rather than individual file metadata, and volume servers, which manage the files and metadata within those volumes directly, which is what enables its fast, constant-time file access at scale. Beyond the free, community-maintained open-source core, there is a commercial SeaweedFS Enterprise edition offered through the separate seaweedfs.com website, which adds advanced features such as data recovery tooling, self-healing storage, customizable erasure coding, and erasure-coding vacuum and repair operations, aimed at organizations that want vendor support and additional operational safeguards on top of the open-source foundation. SeaweedFS Enterprise is billed as a flat per-terabyte software license with no separate request fees and no egress fees, since it runs on the customer's own hardware at local network speed rather than as a hosted cloud service. Published Enterprise pricing includes a monthly option at 2 US dollars per terabyte per month with pay-as-you-go flexibility and cancel-anytime terms, and a yearly option at 20 US dollars per terabyte per year, which represents roughly a 17 percent discount compared to paying monthly and is marketed as the best-value option. Deployments below 25 terabytes are free for development and testing purposes even under the Enterprise licensing model, though production use at any capacity requires a paid license. For very large deployments exceeding 5 petabytes, SeaweedFS offers a custom Enterprise tier with volume discounts, priority support backed by service level agreements, and custom contract terms. Because SeaweedFS does not appear to have a large formally incorporated company with a disclosed headquarters or public employee count behind the open-source project itself, those company-level facts are not independently verifiable and should be treated as unconfirmed rather than stated definitively; the project is best understood as a widely adopted open-source distributed storage system with a lightweight commercial Enterprise offering layered on top.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SeaweedFS is a fast, open-source distributed storage system created by Chris Lu and released under the Apache License, Version 2.0. It has been publicly maintained since around 2015 and has grown to roughly 32,000 stars on GitHub, reflecting strong developer community adoption.

The project supports S3-compatible object storage, general-purpose file access, and Apache Iceberg table storage, and is architected to handle billions of files with O(1) disk seek performance, meaning file access typically requires only a single disk read regardless of total file count.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SeaweedFS separates metadata management from file storage: a lightweight central master tracks only storage volumes, while volume servers manage files and their metadata directly, enabling fast, constant-time file access at massive scale. It supports the S3 API, POSIX FUSE mounts, Hadoop compatibility, WebDAV, encryption, and erasure coding for warm storage.

A separate commercial SeaweedFS Enterprise edition adds advanced data recovery, self-healing storage, customizable erasure coding, and erasure-coding vacuum and repair tooling on top of the open-source core, aimed at organizations that want additional operational safeguards and vendor support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The open-source core of SeaweedFS is free to self-host under the Apache 2.0 license with no licensing fees. SeaweedFS Enterprise is billed as a flat per-terabyte software license running on the customer's own hardware, with no request fees and no egress fees.

Enterprise pricing is 2 US dollars per terabyte per month on a pay-as-you-go monthly plan, or 20 US dollars per terabyte per year on an annual plan, representing roughly a 17 percent discount versus monthly billing. Deployments under 25 terabytes are free for development and testing, and custom pricing with volume discounts is available for deployments exceeding 5 petabytes.

features.title: S3-compatible object storage
features.description: Implements an S3-compatible API so existing S3 tooling and clients can work against a self-hosted SeaweedFS cluster.

features.title: O(1) disk seek architecture
features.description: File access typically requires only a single disk read operation regardless of how many files are stored in the cluster.

features.title: Lightweight master and volume server design
features.description: A central master tracks only storage volumes, while volume servers manage individual files and metadata directly, reducing metadata bottlenecks.

features.title: POSIX FUSE mount and Hadoop compatibility
features.description: Supports mounting as a POSIX filesystem and Hadoop-compatible access for big data workloads.

features.title: Erasure coding for warm storage
features.description: Customizable erasure coding reduces storage overhead for less frequently accessed data compared to full replication.

features.title: Apache Iceberg table support
features.description: Supports storing Iceberg tables, extending SeaweedFS into data lake and analytics use cases.

features.title: Self-healing storage (Enterprise)
features.description: The Enterprise edition adds self-healing capabilities and data recovery tooling for improved operational resilience.

features.title: Flat per-terabyte Enterprise licensing
features.description: Enterprise pricing is a simple per-terabyte fee with no request or egress charges, since it runs on the customer's own infrastructure.

pros: Free, Apache 2.0 licensed open-source core with no licensing cost for self-hosting
Designed for very high file counts with constant-time (O(1)) disk access performance
S3 API compatibility allows reuse of existing S3 tooling and client libraries
Enterprise pricing has no request fees or egress fees since it runs on customer-owned hardware
Free tier for development and testing under 25 terabytes even for the Enterprise edition
cons: No publicly disclosed formal company headquarters or employee count for the project's commercial entity
Production use of the Enterprise edition requires a paid per-terabyte license regardless of scale
Self-hosting and operating a distributed storage cluster requires infrastructure and systems expertise
Smaller ecosystem and third-party integrations compared to established cloud object storage providers
Advanced resilience features like self-healing and erasure-coding repair are limited to the paid Enterprise tier

faqs.question: Is SeaweedFS free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeaweedFS's open-source core is free to self-host under the Apache License, Version 2.0. A separate paid Enterprise edition adds advanced features for a per-terabyte fee.

faqs.question: Who created SeaweedFS?
faqs.answer: SeaweedFS was created by Chris Lu, who has publicly maintained the project since around 2015.

faqs.question: How much does SeaweedFS Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: SeaweedFS Enterprise costs 2 US dollars per terabyte per month on a monthly plan, or 20 US dollars per terabyte per year on an annual plan, which is roughly a 17 percent discount versus monthly billing.

faqs.question: Is SeaweedFS compatible with Amazon S3?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeaweedFS implements an S3-compatible API, allowing existing S3 tools and client libraries to work with a self-hosted SeaweedFS cluster.

faqs.question: What license does SeaweedFS use?
faqs.answer: SeaweedFS is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0.

faqs.question: Is SeaweedFS suitable for storing billions of files?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeaweedFS's architecture is specifically designed to handle billions of files with O(1) disk seek performance, meaning file access typically requires only one disk read operation.

faqs.question: Is there a free tier for SeaweedFS Enterprise?
faqs.answer: Deployments under 25 terabytes are free for development and testing purposes, though production use at any capacity requires a paid Enterprise license.

faqs.question: Does SeaweedFS support data lake workloads?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeaweedFS supports Apache Iceberg table storage in addition to S3-compatible object storage and general file access.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted S3-compatible object storage
use_cases.description: Organizations run SeaweedFS as a self-hosted alternative to cloud object storage providers, avoiding per-request and egress fees.
use_cases.audience: Infrastructure and platform engineering teams

use_cases.title: Storing billions of small files
use_cases.description: Applications that generate huge numbers of small files, such as image or document storage systems, use SeaweedFS for its constant-time disk access architecture.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams building large-scale file storage applications

use_cases.title: Data lake and analytics storage
use_cases.description: Teams use SeaweedFS's Apache Iceberg support to back data lake and analytics workloads on self-managed infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Data engineering teams managing self-hosted data lakes

features: POSIX FUSE mount and Hadoop compatibility

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source Linux distribution for threat hunting, enterprise security monitoring, and log management, bundling tools like Suricata, Zeek, and the Elastic Stack.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free and open source software, with paid training, support, and professional services sold separately
enrichment.starting_price: Free (open-source distribution); paid training and support plans available on request
enrichment.company_size: Small company (Security Onion Solutions, LLC, founded 2014)
enrichment.seo_title: Security Onion Review 2026: Free Threat Hunting Platform Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Security Onion is a free, open-source Linux distro for threat hunting and security monitoring. See features, bundled tools, and paid training and support options.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Security Onion is a free and open-source Linux distribution built for threat hunting, enterprise security monitoring, and log management, originally started as a personal open-source project by Doug Burks in 2008. As the platform grew in popularity, Burks founded Security Onion Solutions, LLC in 2014 to provide official training, support, and professional services around the free software; the company is headquartered at 717 Bishops Circle in Evans, Georgia, USA. Security Onion itself remains completely free and open source, with the source code publicly available, and it is created and maintained by a community of cybersecurity professionals who use it in their own environments. The distribution bundles a curated set of best-of-breed open-source security tools rather than building everything from scratch, including the Suricata intrusion detection and prevention engine, the Zeek network security monitoring framework, and the Elastic Stack for log storage, search, and visualization, alongside its own analyst-facing interfaces for alert triage and case management. Reported adoption figures cited by the project put downloads at over 1,000,000, with installations reported in nearly every country in the world, reflecting broad usage among security operations centers, government agencies, and individual practitioners worldwide. Because Security Onion is free, the company's revenue model is built entirely around optional paid services layered on top of the free distribution: Security Onion Solutions, LLC positions itself as the only official training and support provider for the platform, offering both on-demand training, available in free and premium tiers, and instructor-led training delivered either in person or in virtual lab environments, plus professional support services covering architecture and hardware planning through step-by-step remote deployment assistance at any deployment scale. None of Security Onion Solutions' pricing for training, support, or professional services is published publicly on its website; the site contains no pricing pages, rate cards, or cost estimates, which is typical of custom, quote-based B2B service pricing that varies according to deployment scale, chosen support tier, and specific customer requirements. The company also offers government pricing through a partner, IntelliGenesis, and lists itself as a seller on AWS Marketplace. For an SEO directory context, the most load-bearing, verifiable facts are that the underlying software is entirely free and open source, that the company behind official commercial services, Security Onion Solutions LLC, was founded in 2014 (five years after the software project itself began in 2008) and is headquartered in Evans, Georgia, and that its bundled toolset centers on Suricata, Zeek, and the Elastic Stack.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Security Onion is a free and open-source Linux distribution for threat hunting, enterprise security monitoring, and log management, started by Doug Burks in 2008. As adoption grew, Burks founded Security Onion Solutions, LLC in 2014 to provide official training, support, and professional services around the free platform, headquartered in Evans, Georgia, USA.

The software itself remains completely free and open source, while Security Onion Solutions, LLC generates revenue through paid training, support, and professional services layered on top of the free distribution.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Security Onion bundles best-of-breed open-source security tools rather than reinventing them, including the Suricata intrusion detection and prevention engine, the Zeek network security monitoring framework, and the Elastic Stack for log storage, search, and visualization, combined into a single, ready-to-deploy distribution for security teams.

On top of these bundled tools, Security Onion provides its own analyst interfaces for alert triage, case management, and hunting across network and log data, aimed at giving security operations teams a unified platform rather than requiring them to integrate each open-source component separately.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Security Onion software itself is entirely free and open source, with no licensing cost regardless of deployment scale. Security Onion Solutions, LLC, the official commercial entity behind the project, generates revenue through separate paid offerings.

These paid offerings include on-demand training in free and premium tiers, instructor-led training delivered in person or virtually, and professional support and deployment services. None of this pricing is published publicly; it is quote-based and varies by deployment scale and requirements, with government pricing available through partner IntelliGenesis.

features.title: Free and open-source distribution
features.description: The full Security Onion platform is free to download, install, and use at any scale, with publicly available source code.

features.title: Bundled Suricata IDS/IPS
features.description: Includes the Suricata intrusion detection and prevention engine for network threat detection.

features.title: Bundled Zeek network monitoring
features.description: Includes the Zeek network security monitoring framework for deep network traffic analysis.

features.title: Elastic Stack integration
features.description: Uses the Elastic Stack for centralized log storage, search, and visualization of security data.

features.title: Analyst triage and case management interfaces
features.description: Provides built-in interfaces for security analysts to triage alerts and manage investigation cases.

features.title: Official instructor-led training
features.description: Security Onion Solutions, LLC offers official in-person and virtual instructor-led training courses.

features.title: On-demand training tiers
features.description: Free and premium on-demand training options are available for self-paced learning.

features.title: Professional deployment support
features.description: Paid support services cover architecture and hardware planning through remote deployment assistance at any scale.

pros: Completely free and open-source with no licensing cost at any deployment scale
Bundles proven, widely trusted security tools like Suricata, Zeek, and the Elastic Stack into one distribution
Backed by an official company, Security Onion Solutions LLC, offering legitimate paid training and support
Large reported install base with over 1,000,000 downloads across nearly every country
Government pricing available through partner IntelliGenesis for public sector buyers
cons: No public pricing for official training, support, or professional services, requiring direct contact with sales
Self-managing a Security Onion deployment without paid support requires significant in-house security expertise
As a bundle of multiple complex open-source tools, initial setup and tuning can be resource-intensive
Community-based free support may be slower than paid support tiers for urgent issues
Requires dedicated hardware or infrastructure sizing planning, especially for larger network monitoring deployments

faqs.question: Is Security Onion free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Security Onion is a completely free and open-source Linux distribution. Optional paid training, support, and professional services are available separately from Security Onion Solutions, LLC.

faqs.question: Who created Security Onion?
faqs.answer: Security Onion was started as an open-source project by Doug Burks in 2008. He later founded Security Onion Solutions, LLC in 2014 to provide official commercial training and support.

faqs.question: Where is Security Onion Solutions headquartered?
faqs.answer: Security Onion Solutions, LLC is headquartered at 717 Bishops Circle, Evans, Georgia, USA.

faqs.question: What tools are bundled with Security Onion?
faqs.answer: Security Onion bundles the Suricata intrusion detection and prevention engine, the Zeek network security monitoring framework, and the Elastic Stack for log storage, search, and visualization, along with its own analyst interfaces.

faqs.question: Does Security Onion offer official training?
faqs.answer: Yes. Security Onion Solutions, LLC is the only official training provider, offering both on-demand training in free and premium tiers and instructor-led training delivered in person or virtually.

faqs.question: How much does Security Onion support cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing for Security Onion Solutions' support and training is not published publicly; it is quote-based and depends on deployment scale, support level, and specific requirements.

faqs.question: How widely is Security Onion used?
faqs.answer: The project reports over 1,000,000 downloads with installations in nearly every country in the world.

faqs.question: Is Security Onion suitable for enterprise security monitoring?
faqs.answer: Yes. Security Onion is designed for threat hunting, enterprise security monitoring, and log management, and is used by security operations centers of varying sizes worldwide.

use_cases.description: Small IT and security teams use Security Onion's free distribution to gain enterprise-grade monitoring capability without software licensing costs.

use_cases.title: Hands-on cybersecurity training and labs

use_cases.audience: Cybersecurity students and professionals developing hands-on skills
